summary: The editable gallery is structurally correct and asset-backed, but the featured workbench is too tall and sparse: the chart sits in a large blank panel and the widget cards begin below the ideal first-viewport position.

majorDiffAreas.title: Featured workbench is too tall and pushes the gallery down
majorDiffAreas.expected: The source image keeps a compact interactive workbench and shows a full row of widget cards in the first viewport.
majorDiffAreas.actual: The editable mockup has a large empty feature visual area and only the top edge of the card grid is visible at the bottom of the 1440x900 capture.
majorDiffAreas.impact: The page reads less like a dense visual gallery and more like a hero with unused whitespace, weakening resource discovery.

evidence.observation: In the actual screenshot, the feature image occupies a tall pale panel with large blank space below the plot; gallery cards start near the bottom edge.
The final report detects repeated rows in the right rail but not the lower widget grid as a prominent region.

majorDiffAreas.recommendedChange: Compress the hero/workbench vertical rhythm, reduce the blank feature-visual panel, use a shorter feature image, and pull the controls/card grid upward.

matches: Overall header, hero, workbench, right learning path, tabs, and gallery structure are present
Palette and academic paper treatment are close enough for this pass
possibleIntentionalDeviations: Real GIF widgets replace generated chart stills by design

inputQuality: Both captures are readable and same viewport

nextIteration.focus: Tighten first-viewport density for the editable visual gallery.
steps: Reduce workbench min-height and feature panel padding
avoidForNow: Do not change assets or invent new screenshots

rationale: The target is already editable and asset-backed, so a CSS-only density pass is the highest-value next improvement.
